title: Adjectives in context (A2)
lesson: Choose the adjective that best describes each situation. These words help you describe places, people, and feelings more precisely.

items.text: The film was so {1} that I fell asleep.
items.explanation: Boring means not interesting and making you tired.

items.text: The train was very {1}, with no free seats at all.
items.explanation: Crowded means full of people.

items.text: This is the most {1} chair I have ever sat in.
items.explanation: Comfortable means pleasant and easy to relax in.

items.text: Swimming in that fast river is very {1}.
items.explanation: Dangerous means likely to cause harm.

items.text: The waiter was very {1} and said thank you to everyone.
items.explanation: Polite means having good manners.

items.text: The library is a {1} place to study.
items.explanation: Quiet means with little or no noise.

items.text: The two brothers look very {1}; they are almost the same.
items.explanation: Similar means almost the same as something else.

items.text: This map is very {1} for finding our way.
items.explanation: Useful means helpful for doing something.

items.text: Our new neighbours are very {1} and always say hello.
items.explanation: Friendly means kind and pleasant to others.

items.text: That singer is very {1}; everyone knows her songs.
items.explanation: Popular means liked by many people.

items.text: Be careful, the road is {1} after the rain.
items.explanation: Slippery means smooth and easy to slip on.

items.text: I am {1} in learning more about history.
items.explanation: Interested means wanting to know more about something.

items.text: The lecture was really {1}; I learned a lot.
items.explanation: Interesting means holding your attention.

items.text: She was {1} after the long flight and needed sleep.
items.explanation: Exhausted means extremely tired.

items.text: The instructions were {1}, so nobody understood them.
items.explanation: Confusing means hard to understand.

items.text: After the explanation, everything was {1} to me.
items.explanation: Clear means easy to understand.

items.text: He felt {1} before his big exam.
items.explanation: Nervous means worried and not relaxed.

items.text: She stayed {1} even when the others were panicking.
items.explanation: Calm means relaxed and not worried.

items.text: This jacket is {1}; it keeps me warm in winter.
items.explanation: Warm means having a pleasant amount of heat.

items.text: The shopping mall was {1} on Saturday because of the sale.
items.explanation: Busy means full of activity or people.

items.text: After cleaning all day, the house looked very {1}.
items.explanation: Tidy means neat and well organised.

items.text: His desk is so {1} that he can never find anything.
items.explanation: Messy means untidy and disordered.

items.text: The medicine was {1} and made the patient feel better.
items.explanation: Effective means working well and getting results.

items.text: The story was so {1} that everyone laughed.
items.explanation: Funny means making you laugh.

items.text: It is {1} to wear a helmet when you ride a bike.
items.explanation: Sensible means showing good judgement.

items.text: The hotel staff were very {1} and carried our bags.
items.explanation: Helpful means willing to help and being useful.

items.text: The weather is {1} today, with clear skies and sun.
items.explanation: Pleasant means nice and enjoyable.

items.text: Diamonds are very {1}, so few people can buy them.
items.explanation: Rare means not found often.

items.text: Mobile phones are very {1} now; nearly everyone has one.
items.explanation: Common means found or happening often.

items.text: The cake was {1} and tasted wonderful.
items.explanation: Delicious means having a very good taste.

items.text: The water in the lake was so {1} we could see the bottom.
items.explanation: Clear water means you can see through it easily.

items.text: He is very {1} and always gives money to charity.
items.explanation: Generous means happy to give and share.

items.text: The exam questions were {1}, so most students passed.
items.explanation: Simple means easy to do or understand.

items.text: Their flat is quite {1}, with only one small bedroom.
items.explanation: Tiny means extremely small.
